Fleet management-services runtime - one of TWO same-labelled services-runtime FQDNs (lowercase FQDN)
Hosted fleet-level components - e.g. fleet lifecycle (lowercase FQDN)
Hosted instance-level components - e.g. SDDC lifecycle + real-time metrics (lowercase FQDN)
Fleet identity broker (deployed at bring-up; configured Day-N; lowercase FQDN; IP from the services-runtime block)
Automation (the one fleet component you can defer to Day-N; lowercase FQDN). IP must fall OUTSIDE the VCF services runtime nodes /29 you enter in the wizard - a discrete VM Mgmt IP

Automation's OWN services runtime - a second FQDN with the same label as the fleet one above (lowercase FQDN). Also OUTSIDE the /29: the wizard states both Automation FQDNs must resolve outside the provided CIDR

Contiguous /29 from the VM Mgmt subnet - 3 node IPs + 2 buffer; entered as VCF services runtime nodes CIDR in the Add VCF Automation wizard. IP-only - no DNS records. Contiguous /29 required on 9.1.0.0-9.1.0.300; from 9.1.0.400 TechDocs asks only for 5 unique IPs. Must not overlap the VCF Management Services runtime block
Log Management integrated cluster VIP (lowercase FQDN; VIP + 6 worker IPs +2 per extra replica all come from the services-runtime block - size it /27)

VCF Operations for Networks platform node. NOT needed at bring-up - Day-N only. The wizard asks for an IP address ONLY (no FQDN field; TechDocs marks FQDN N/A) - an A + PTR is optional but recommended for runbooks / firewall rules / log identification. A Large platform is a 3-node cluster (+2 IPs)
VCF Operations for Networks collector node - same story - IP-only in the wizard with the FQDN optional. NOT needed at bring-up
Avi LB cluster FQDN + VIP (only if Avi in scope; the 3 controller nodes are IP-only - no DNS records)
Per Supervisor-enabled workload domain - resolves to the control-plane floating IP (or the LB VIP); FQDN login is required
Third-site witness (stretched / multi-AZ only) - deployed for the stretch step after bring-up
